_readme: THE CONTROL PANEL for your Daily Brief. Everything the morning routine does is driven by this file. Edit it freely — no code changes needed for the common cases. See brief/README.md for the full guide. Three things you'll touch most: (1) `modules` — the domains that get scanned and the exact `queries`/`sources`/`lenses` behind each; (2) `layout.sections` — what appears in the brief and in what order; (3) `delivery` — where the finished brief is sent.

owner.name: Anthony M. Carrella
owner.voice: Warm, literate, unhurried. A community leader with soul — faith, arts, civic life, and ideas held together. Prefers signal over noise, one good thing to sit with over ten to skim.

schedule._note: Cron is in the routine's timezone. This is the wake time for the generation session. To change the time, edit this AND update the routine (see README → Scheduling).

format.title: The Daily Brief
format.subtitle: for Anthony — {longdate}
format._note: maxItemsPerModule caps how many items each scanner surfaces. recencyWindowHours defines 'news' — items older than this are only kept if evergreen (e.g. an exhibition still on view).

modules.label: Faith & Ministry
queries: Presbyterian Church USA news
PCUSA General Assembly OR polity OR statement
Overbrook Presbyterian Church Philadelphia
theology essays contemplative practice community
faith and community organizing city
Revised Common Lectionary readings this week

sources.detail: general web + news search on the queries
Presbyterian Outlook, The Christian Century, PCUSA News, Faith & Leadership (Duke)

lenses: One piece of denominational or church-world news worth knowing
One idea to sit with — a line of theology, a practice, a provocation
A lectionary / scripture note for the day or week, if relevant to preaching or reflection
modules.agentBrief: You scan the faith & ministry world for Anthony, a Presbyterian community leader in Philadelphia. Favor thoughtful, substantive, non-sensational sources. Surface things that would enrich a sermon, a session meeting, a pastoral conversation, or his own spiritual life. Avoid culture-war bait and shallow hot takes. When you cite scripture or a lectionary reading, name the reference precisely.

modules.label: Arts & Culture
queries: Whitney Museum new exhibition contemporary American art
Philadelphia Museum of Art OR Barnes Foundation new exhibition
notable poetry essay OR poem published this week
film criticism new release worth seeing
contemporary art review New York Philadelphia

sources.detail: Whitney Museum open-access: what's on view, exhibitions, artworks. Preserve attribution + whitney.org links.
Optional: one new/relevant release or a themed listen.
arts & culture press for exhibitions, film, letters

lenses: What's on view / newly opened worth knowing about
One work or artist to contemplate today (with image + attribution when from Whitney)
One cultural piece — an essay, poem, review — worth reading in full
modules.agentBrief: You are Anthony's arts & culture scout. He has a real connection to the Whitney and to American art, and a literary sensibility. Prefer depth: one work seen well beats five names dropped. Use the Whitney MCP for anything you can (always keep the artist attribution and the whitney.org link). Reach beyond the museum too — poetry, film, letters, music.

modules.label: Community & Civic Leadership
queries: Philadelphia civic news neighborhoods West Philadelphia Overbrook
nonprofit leadership OR community organizing best practices
social impact philanthropy city initiative
leadership essay management wisdom
Philadelphia city council OR mayor policy community

sources.detail: local Philadelphia news (Inquirer, WHYY/Billy Penn), civic & nonprofit press, leadership writing

lenses: One piece of local / civic news that actually matters to his community
One leadership or organizing idea worth carrying into the week
An opportunity, event, or convening on the horizon to put on the radar
modules.agentBrief: You track community & civic life for Anthony, a Philadelphia community leader. Ground it locally — West Philadelphia, Overbrook, the city — but include leadership thinking that travels. Prefer the constructive and the actionable over the merely alarming. Flag concrete opportunities (grants, convenings, events) he might act on.

modules.label: Tech, AI & Tools
queries: AI agents tooling for knowledge work practical
Anthropic Claude new capability OR release
thoughtful technology criticism essay
productivity tool for writers and community leaders
AI in ministry OR nonprofits OR the arts

sources.detail: tech & AI press, but filtered hard for signal — releases that matter, ideas worth thinking about, tools worth trying

lenses: One development in AI / tech that's genuinely signal, not hype
One tool or capability worth trying this week
One idea about technology and human life worth adding to the radar
modules.agentBrief: You filter tech & AI for Anthony, who is thoughtful but not a full-time technologist. He wants signal, not noise: what actually changed, what's worth trying, what idea is worth holding. Explain plainly, no jargon dumps. Especially flag anything that could serve ministry, the arts, community work, or his own writing.

modules.label: Serendipity
queries: surprising idea from history OR science OR philosophy
wonder curiosity beautiful thing in the world
obscure fascinating fact well-sourced

sources.detail: anything genuinely expansive and well-sourced — the point is to widen the horizon, not to inform on a beat

lenses: One delightful, unexpected thing to widen the day — an idea, a fact, a piece of the world he wasn't looking for
modules.agentBrief: Your job is wonder. Bring Anthony one thing he would never have gone looking for — from any field — that makes the world feel larger. It must be true and traceable to a real source. Delight over utility here.

personalContext._note: Pulls from Anthony's own connected accounts to open the brief with 'your actual day'. Requires the relevant connectors to be authorized. Each is optional and degrades gracefully if a connector is offline.

calendar.detail: Today's events (and anything notable tomorrow). Summarize as a clean agenda, not a raw dump.

inbox.detail: Only surface threads that genuinely need attention today — unanswered asks, time-sensitive items. Never quote sensitive content in the published web artifact; keep specifics to the private delivery channels (email/Slack).

layout._note: The brief is assembled from these sections IN THIS ORDER. Each entry names a section `kind` the web renderer understands (see static/brief/js/app.js → RENDERERS) and, for module sections, which module feeds it. Reorder, remove, or add entries to change the brief's shape. To invent a brand-new kind of section, add a renderer with the same key.

sections.title: For the Radar
sections._note: Slower-horizon items the scanners flagged as worth watching but not acting on today.

delivery._note: The web artifact is the canonical output and is always produced. The other channels are layered on top and each can be toggled independently. `visibility: private` sections (like your inbox/agenda) are included in email/Slack but stripped from the public web artifact.

web._note: Writes latest.json + a dated archive file + updates index.json, then commits to main so GitHub Pages redeploys. This is what makes it a live web artifact.

email._note: mode 'draft' creates a Gmail draft you review before it sends; switch to 'send' once you trust it. Renders the full brief (including private sections) as HTML with a link to the live version. Requires the Gmail connector to be authorized.

slack._note: Posts the headline + section highlights + link to the live brief as a DM to yourself. Requires the Slack connector.

pdf._note: The web artifact carries a print stylesheet, so 'Save as PDF' from the browser already works. For automated PDF, a future exporter can drive headless Chromium (pre-installed here) against the live URL. See brief/exporters/README.md.

screenshots._note: Planned: headless-Chromium capture of each section as an image (for sharing). See brief/exporters/README.md.

thymer._note: Planned: export the brief as a Thymer note. No Thymer connector is present yet; the exporter stub documents the two paths (API when available, or Markdown drop-in). See brief/exporters/README.md.

guardrails.sourcesPolicy: Reputable, mainstream, or clearly-credentialed sources only. No sketchy aggregators, no engagement-bait, no unverified rumor. When a claim matters, it must trace to a nameable source with a link.
guardrails.verification: Cross-check anything surprising or consequential against a second source before it goes in the brief. Mark uncertainty honestly ('reported', 'unconfirmed') rather than laundering it into fact.
guardrails.privacy: Personal context (inbox, calendar) never appears in the public web artifact — only in the private delivery channels. Never publish anyone's private information.
guardrails.tone: No doom-scrolling. Constructive, curious, humane. The brief should leave him more oriented and a little more alive to the world, not more anxious.
